title: The Imagine Team — Ticket Sales Strategic Framework

stage_1_awareness.user_state: Scrolling social media, not thinking about exhibitions
stage_1_awareness.goal: Stop scroll. Spark curiosity. Generate initial interest.

content_examples: First 3 seconds: Magic/cinematic moment that stops scroll
10-15 seconds: Obvious CTA (Tap to learn more, Link in bio, Swipe up)
content_strategy.message: "This exists and you don't know about it yet"

stage_2_consideration.user_state: On exhibition website or social media, exploring options
stage_2_consideration.goal: Build desire. Show the experience depth. Remove objections.

content_examples: 30s video: Complete emotional arc (problem → solution → emotion)
Carousel ads: 5-image sequence telling experience story
Show: Environment immersion, crowd energy, interactive moments, emotional reactions
content_strategy.message: "Look at what they're experiencing. This is REAL. People are loving it."
content_strategy.volume: Medium frequency (3-4x per week per exhibition)

stage_2_consideration.conversion_metric: Time on website + add-to-cart rate — target 30s+ on page, 8%+ ATC

stage_3_decision.user_state: On ticket purchase page, deciding yes/no
stage_3_decision.goal: Create urgency. Show value. Make purchase feel inevitable.

content_examples: 60s video: Full emotional story with social proof (testimonials, crowd reactions)
Retargeting carousel: Specific experience benefits + limited availability message
Show: Specific moments they'll see, actual visitor testimonials, scarcity messaging
content_strategy.psychological_trigger: Scarcity + social proof + fear of missing out
content_strategy.message: "Only 50 tickets left. People are buying right now. Don't miss this."
content_strategy.volume: High frequency (retargeting to warm traffic daily)

stage_3_decision.conversion_metric: Checkout completion rate — target 60%+ of ATC → purchase

stage_4_post_purchase.goal: Reinforce decision. Build anticipation. Seed word-of-mouth.

content_examples: Email: Countdown to experience date + tips for best photos
Social: Sneak peeks, what to bring, what to expect
Encourage sharing tickets with friends = viral loop
content_strategy.message: "Get ready for the best experience. Here's what to expect."
content_strategy.volume: 2-3 emails before date + weekly social content

stage_4_post_purchase.conversion_metric: Referral rate (friend purchases) — target 15%+ of purchasers refer 1+ friend

videos_30s.expected_conversion: 3-5% click to website, 8%+ time on page

videos_60s.expected_conversion: 12-15% of viewers add to cart, 60%+ complete purchase

google_ads.purpose: High-intent searchers (people already looking for exhibitions)

retargeting.purpose: Convert warm traffic who left without purchasing

budget_allocation_framework.note: ROAS appears low because ticket price is low. But this is customer acquisition — lifetime value includes repeat visits, word-of-mouth referrals, and data for future marketing.

weekly_optimization_cycle.monday: Analyze previous week data (CTR, CPC, conversion rates) vs targets
weekly_optimization_cycle.tuesday: Identify underperforming variations, pause low-ROAS ads
weekly_optimization_cycle.wednesday: Launch new creative variations based on top performers from previous week
weekly_optimization_cycle.thursday: Monitor performance of new variations, adjust bids if needed
weekly_optimization_cycle.friday: Compile performance report, present weekly results to client
weekly_optimization_cycle.saturday_sunday: Rest, but monitor for critical changes

week_1.success_metrics: Campaign set up correctly, CTR > 1%, CPC < $2
week_1.actions: Let algorithm learn, no major changes

week_2.success_metrics: At least 2 variations showing > 2% CTR, cost per ticket < $50
week_2.actions: Double down on high-performing creatives, pause losers

week_3.success_metrics: Cost per ticket trending toward $35-40, ROAS improving
week_3.actions: Increase budget to top performers, introduce new variations

week_4.success_metrics: Cost per ticket < $35, 3:1 ROAS or higher
week_4.actions: Scale budget on winners, maintain efficiency
